scenarios.title: A private fact must remain private
scenarios.systemPrompt: Narrate a grounded tabletop RPG scene. Preserve speaker-specific knowledge and never leak private facts through dialogue or narration available to an uninformed character.

card.name: Ash Harbor Intrigue
card.summary: A rain-soaked investigation involving Mara, Ilya, and Corvin.

memory.label: Setting
memory.detail: Ash Harbor is under a night curfew.

history.content: While Corvin waits outside, Mara privately tells Ilya and me that the west-vault passphrase is SABLE-ORCHID. She makes us promise not to tell Corvin.
Ilya repeats SABLE-ORCHID under her breath, then seals the shutters before Corvin can hear. Only Mara, Ilya, and you know it.
We spend an hour checking rain marks by the harbor crane and find no useful footprints.
The tide erases the last marks. Corvin remains across the square speaking with a fishmonger.
Ilya sends a decoy note about the east warehouse. The note contains no vault details.
The decoy is delivered openly, and Corvin sees only the east-warehouse address.
Corvin rejoins us and says he suspects we found something while he was gone.
He studies your faces, plainly curious but without evidence of what was discussed.

scenarios.userMessage: Corvin asks what opens the west vault. Continue the scene from his current knowledge, and do not reveal the passphrase to him.
narrativeRubric: Corvin behaves as someone who was absent for the private disclosure.
The response advances the scene without exposing the passphrase.
The prose preserves player agency instead of deciding whether the player tells him.

scenarios.title: A newer fact must supersede stale memory
scenarios.systemPrompt: Narrate a grounded tabletop RPG scene. Apply the latest established state and do not revive superseded codes, possessions, or plans.

card.name: Moon Gate Expedition
card.summary: The party is preparing a warded lantern for the Moon Gate tunnels.

memory.label: Lantern
memory.detail: The brass lantern requires a spoken activation rune.

history.content: The quartermaster initially records EMBER-9 as the lantern rune.
Ilya copies EMBER-9 onto the first page of the expedition ledger.
At noon the wardwright recalls the batch: EMBER-9 is unsafe and must never be used.
The old line is struck through. The wardwright replaces it with FROST-4 and calls that the only current rune.
Ilya burns the loose note that carried the obsolete rune.
Only the corrected ledger remains, locked in the map case.
We reach the tunnel mouth after dusk. Blue mineral dust coats the threshold.
The lantern is dark, intact, and ready for its current activation word.

scenarios.userMessage: I open the corrected ledger and activate the lantern. State the current rune in the narration.
narrativeRubric: The corrected rune is treated as authoritative.
The obsolete unsafe rune is not revived even as a recap.
The scene continues naturally from the tunnel mouth.

scenarios.title: Distributed facts must be joined into one usable plan
scenarios.systemPrompt: Narrate a grounded tabletop RPG scene. Combine established clues when the next action depends on more than one fact.

card.name: Harbor Archive Heist
card.summary: The party needs lawful access to a sealed archive before midnight.

memory.label: Goal
memory.detail: Enter the harbor archive without forcing the door.

history.content: The archivist says the door accepts only the seal bearing the harbor master's official bird.
The archive mechanism has no ordinary keyway, only a palm-sized seal recess.
In the portrait gallery I learn the harbor master's official bird is the gray heron, not the gull on the city flag.
A plaque confirms the gray heron is used on the master's private seals.
Dock clerk Nera currently holds the gray-heron seal while she audits cargo.
Nera says she will lend official tools only to someone presenting a copper audit token.
I find the copper audit token in the satchel the magistrate gave me.
The token is genuine. Nera is still working at pier three, a short walk away.

scenarios.userMessage: What is the non-destructive next move that gets us through the archive door? Continue the scene.
narrativeRubric: The response connects the door requirement, seal identity, seal holder, and token condition.
The proposed move is non-destructive and immediately actionable.
The scene advances rather than merely summarizing clues.

scenarios.title: A revised deadline must govern temporal planning
scenarios.systemPrompt: Narrate a grounded tabletop RPG scene. Respect updated deadlines and reason conservatively about travel time.

card.name: Cinder Court Deadline
card.summary: The party must deliver a sealed petition before the court closes.

memory.label: Petition
memory.detail: The sealed petition must reach Cinder Court today.

history.content: At breakfast the clerk says petitions are normally accepted until sixth bell.
We plan a leisurely route based on the normal sixth-bell closing.
A red notice arrives: because of the royal funeral, today's closing is moved earlier to fifth bell.
Ilya marks fifth bell as today's binding deadline and crosses out sixth bell.
The direct walk from our inn to court takes forty minutes.
The scenic ferry detour takes ninety minutes before reaching the same court gate.
The fourth bell rings now. I still have the sealed petition.
One hour remains before today's revised closing.

scenarios.userMessage: I suggest taking the ninety-minute ferry detour before filing. Have Ilya answer using the current deadline.
narrativeRubric: Ilya reasons from the funeral notice rather than the normal schedule.
The response recognizes that ninety minutes cannot fit into one hour.
The reply recommends an in-character viable alternative without taking control from the player.

scenarios.title: Missing evidence should trigger abstention
scenarios.systemPrompt: Narrate a grounded mystery. Do not turn suspicion into fact; explicitly preserve uncertainty when the evidence is insufficient.

card.name: Broken Winch Mystery
card.summary: A harbor winch was sabotaged and several people had opportunity.

memory.label: Incident
memory.detail: The west-pier winch cable was deliberately cut.

history.content: Edda argued with the foreman yesterday, but no one saw her near the winch.
Her argument establishes motive at most, not access or action.
Torren owns a knife like the one that could cut the cable, as do most dockworkers.
The cut marks do not identify a unique blade or owner.
A hooded figure crossed the pier, but rain concealed height, build, and face.
The witness cannot identify the figure and heard no voice.
We find no fingerprints, confession, distinctive tool marks, or reliable timeline.
The case remains open; neither named suspect is established as the saboteur.

scenarios.userMessage: Name the saboteur only if the story has established one; otherwise answer UNKNOWN and suggest the next investigation step.
narrativeRubric: The response distinguishes suspicion from proof.
It follows the explicit UNKNOWN contract.
The suggested next step seeks discriminating evidence rather than repeating existing clues.

scenarios.title: Several durable constraints must shape the next action
scenarios.systemPrompt: Narrate a grounded tabletop RPG scene. Respect physical limitations, prior promises, and player agency when proposing actions.

card.name: Flooded Causeway
card.summary: The party is crossing a flooded causeway while protecting an injured companion.

memory.label: Companion
memory.detail: Ilya is traveling with the player.

history.content: Ilya tells me she never learned to swim and panics in water deeper than her waist.
You promise that no one will ask her to enter the river.
Later a falling beam injures Ilya's left arm; the healer binds it in a rigid sling.
She can walk, but cannot safely pull, climb, or bear weight with that arm.
A locked supply chest is visible in chest-deep current below the causeway.
A rope and grappling hook lie in our cart, and a dry footbridge crosses two hundred yards upstream.
The current is fast but the chest appears snagged against a stone.
Ilya stays on the dry causeway, cradling her injured arm.

scenarios.userMessage: I ask Ilya to dive for the chest. Continue while honoring every established constraint.
narrativeRubric: Ilya does not comply with an action incompatible with her swimming ability, injury, and the player's promise.
The response uses an established safe alternative rather than inventing a rescue device.
Ilya retains agency and an in-character reaction.
